mechanism_narrative: A rising 3-month trend in FEDFUNDS increases equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month changes in FEDFUNDS are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0 while CPIAUCSL is still rising over 3 months, markets price restrictive policy persistence, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with persistent inflation
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with FEDFUNDS above 4.0 and positive 3-month CPIAUCSL change do not produce below-baseline 5-day S&P 500 returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and recession risk, leading investors to reduce equity exposure and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by nonnegative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of observations or by an average return not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST indicate broad consumer and housing demand strength, supporting earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both have positive 3-month growth
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous positive 3-month growth in RSAFS and HOUST does not raise mean 20-day S&P 500 returns above unconditional mean in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A higher 3-month change in INDPRO signals improving production and operating leverage for listed firms, increasing expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the walk-forward Rank IC between 3-month INDPRO change and 20-day S&P 500 returns is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Low UNRATE combined with strong PAYEMS growth signals an overheated labor market that keeps FEDFUNDS restrictive, compressing equity multiples and lowering S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 4.0 and PAYEMS 3-month growth is positive
regime_hint: late-cycle tight labor market with restrictive policy
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of UNRATE below 4.0 and positive PAYEMS growth is followed by above-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST leads construction and durable-goods weakness, reducing cyclicals' earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines greater than 10% over 3 months are not followed by lower-than-baseline 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth crossing above a 2% annualized pace confirms resilient aggregate demand, improving earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth crosses above 2%
failure_condition: Reject if GDPC1 growth crossings above 2% annualized do not produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns more often than the unconditional hit rate.
